question: Is Novo Nordisk's revenue decline (-7.6% YoY) temporary or structural?

findings.claim: Novo struck a Most Favored Nation pricing deal with the Trump administration, cutting Ozempic/Wegovy prices to $350/month via TrumpRx (from ~$1,000-1,350 list)
findings.data: Under the MFN deal: Ozempic and Wegovy available at $350/month through TrumpRx.gov (launched Feb 5, 2026). Medicare price: $245/month, with copays capped at $50. Starting Jan 1, 2027, list prices for Wegovy, Ozempic, and Rybelsus will be $675 across the board — a 50% cut for Wegovy (from $1,349) and 34% cut for Ozempic/Rybelsus (from $1,027). Both Novo and Lilly received 3 years of tariff relief in exchange.

findings.claim: Cash-pay pricing already dropped 30%: from $499 to $349/month, with first 2 months for new patients at $199
findings.data: Novo reduced its direct-to-consumer pricing to $349/month for Ozempic and Wegovy (from $499), with $199 intro pricing for new patients' first two months at low doses. This predates the MFN deal and reflects competitive pressure from compounders and Eli Lilly.

findings.claim: Hims & Hers announced partnership with Novo to distribute branded Wegovy and Ozempic at $149/month
findings.data: On March 18, 2026, Hims & Hers opened a waitlist for branded Wegovy and Ozempic at $149/month via a direct deal with Novo Nordisk. This DTC channel bypasses PBMs entirely and represents potentially the lowest-ever price for branded semaglutide.

findings.claim: Management said MFN pricing impact on global sales growth is 'low single digit' negative — suggesting volume could offset most price cuts
findings.data: Novo told investors the combined effect of U.S. price negotiations and new discount programs should have only a 'low single digit' negative impact on global sales growth in 2026. This implies management believes expanded access will drive volume growth to substantially offset lower prices.
